response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of broad software engineering experience but only ~6 months of professional C/C++ work (National Instruments, 2014–2015) and a university-era C++ project. The role requires deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial-automation domain knowledge (IEC 62443, MQTT, Modbus/OPC UA, message brokers), none of which appear on the resume. Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang in web, fintech, and direct-mail domains — a poor match for a senior C++ industrial-automation role.
strengths: Long tenure (15+ years) with senior/staff titles and demonstrated ownership
Strong testing/TDD culture, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Exposure to distributed, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic micro-services, event-driven GIS platform)
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience — last hands-on C++ role was ~11 years ago for only ~6 months
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
Currently on a career gap and prior roles are web/fintech-oriented, not industrial automation or embedded platforms

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / MQTT background
Senior-level engineering breadth and mentoring track record
